description: Configuration for high availability clustering; as well as defining external configuration and runtime database connection properties.

cluster.description: Configuration for Verify Identity Access High Availability cluster nodes.

dsc.description: Configuration for the Verify Identity Access Distributed Session Cache.

Database.description: External database configuration. PKI required to connect to databases should be defined in the the `ssl_certificates` property.

ssl_keystore.description: SSL database to use to verify connections. Only valid if ssl == true.

user.description: Username to authenticate to database as.

db_name.description: Name of the database instance to use.

sig_file.description: Signature file generated by the primary master; used to add nodes to the cluster.

primary_master.description: Verify Identity Access appliance designated as the primary master node.

secondary_master.description: Verify Identity Access appliance designated as the secondary master node.

nodes.description: Verify Identity Access appliance designated as nodes.

restricted_nodes.description: Verify Identity Access appliance designated as the restricted nodes.

external_clients.description: A flag indicating whether clients that are external to the cluster will need to use the DSC.

port.description: The port over which DSC communication will take place. This parameter is required if external_clients is set to true.

use_ssl.description: A flag indicating whether or not SSL should be used when communicating with the DSC. This parameter is required if external_clients is set to true.

ssl_keyfile.description: The keyfile that will house the SSL certificates used by the DSC. This parameter is required if use_ssl is set to true.

ssl_label.description: The name of the SSL certificate that will be presented to clients. This parameter is optional and can only be set if ssl_keyfile is set.

ssl_ciphers.description: Comma separated list of SSL ciphers permitted for use in TLS connections to the DSC. Valid values include: "RSA_WITH_SHA224", "RSA_WITH_SHA256", "RSA_WITH_SHA384", "RSA_WITH_SHA512", "ECDSA_WITH_SHA224", "ECDSA_WITH_SHA256", "ECDSA_WITH_SHA384", and "ECDSA_WITH_SHA512".

tls_12_cipher_specs.description: Comma separated list of TLS 1.2 cipher specs permitted for use in TLS connections to the DSC.

tls_13_cipher_specs.description: Comma separated list of TLS 1.3 cipher specs permitted for use in TLS connections to the DSC.

worker_threads.description: The number of worker threads that will be used.

maximum_session_lifetime.description: The maximum lifetime of sessions within the DSC.

maximum_session_list.description: The maximum number of sessions to return when a "listSessions" request is made to the DSC. Note this may have an impact on performance.

client_grace_period.description: When a client is shut down we give the client a grace period (in seconds) to restart and register an interest in a session again before we remove the session from the session cache. This will give the client a chance to restart without losing sessions from the server. The value specified here should be similar to the idle timeout value for the session.

connection_idle_timeout.description: The maximum length of time that a connection from a client can remain idle before it is closed by the server. A value of 0 indicates that connections will not be reused. The default value is 0.
